prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for phenol (13 atoms: C₆H₅OH) using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit RKS) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 for the neutral species (cation: charge +1 mult 2; anion: charge −1 mult 2). The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz with no re-optimization (single-point Fukui via finite difference of Mulliken charges).

All three SCF states (N, N+1, N−1) converged. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. Integrity: trustworthy = true. No engine warnings reported.

Headline result (most electrophilic site): Carbon atom 3 (1-based), f⁺ = 2.1028319. Most nucleophilic site: Oxygen atom 1, f⁻ = 0.16201477.

The ortho/para carbons (atoms 3, 4, 7) carry the largest f⁺ values (+2.10, +1.62, +1.31), consistent with phenol's known activation of the aromatic ring toward electrophilic aromatic substitution at ortho and para positions by the –OH group. The oxygen is the dominant nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.162).

CLI invocation: cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpab8zicwh/live.json

Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260702-222029_phenol_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
